source: NvChad - NvChad UI: Plugins Manager: lazy.nvim's role, checkhealth, and inserting/removing a plugin

questions.q: Which plugin manages plugins in NvChad 2.0?
questions.why: lazy.nvim is installed during the bootstrap and manages all plugins.

questions.q: A key startup benefit of lazy.nvim is that it...
options: automatically checks for and installs missing plugins
questions.why: Missing plugins are auto-installed, handy when moving a config to a new machine.

questions.q: Which command runs lazy.nvim's environment health check?
questions.why: :checkhealth lazy reports git, package managers and warnings.

questions.q: How do you add a NEW plugin?
questions.why: New plugins are added by editing lua/plugins/init.lua.

questions.q: After you save a changed plugins file, lazy.nvim shows...
questions.why: Answer yes to reload without leaving the editor.

questions.q: In the :Lazy interface, which key installs a recognized plugin?
To remove a plugin you delete its block from init.lua, then in :Lazy press...
questions.why: Removal (cleaning the folder) is done with X.

questions.q: How do you open lazy.nvim's inline help?
questions.why: :Lazy help or ? shows navigation and controls.
